topStories.title: Quote of the Day by Charlie Chaplin: ‘You’ll never find a rainbow if…'
topStories.description: Charlie Chaplin's timeless words — 'You'll never find a rainbow if you're looking down' — remind us that hope is a direction, not just a feeling. Look up, and life's beauty reveals itself.

topStories.title: Central govt must not treat states as subordinates: SC Justice Nagarathna
topStories.description: Supreme Court Justice BV Nagarathna said the central government should treat states as equals, promoting cooperative federalism over hierarchical governance.

dailyNews.title: Quote of the day by Bo Bennett on making mistakes: ‘Our mistakes don’t define us, it is how we…'
dailyNews.description: The quote tells us to not get bowed down by the mistakes we make – doesn't matter where – because that's not what defines us. What defines us is how we learn from those mistakes.

dailyNews.title: Attacks on nuclear facilities ‘will end life’ in gulf cities not Tehran: Iran’s FM after US-Israel strikes on Bushehr
dailyNews.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi warned that further strikes on the Bushehr nuclear power plant could lead to radioactive fallout affecting GCC capitals. He slammed Western silence over the attacks, which he claims have occurred multiple times.

dailyNews.title: Congress leader Ibobi urges Centre to delay Manipur census till normalcy is restored
dailyNews.description: Ibobi Singh urges Centre to defer Census in Manipur, citing displacement, data accuracy concerns and ongoing law and order challenges.

dailyNews.title: Quote of the Day: John D. Rockefeller urges bold choices — ‘Don’t be afraid to give up…’
dailyNews.description: Rockefeller’s words remind us to think big and act with courage. They encourage people to trust their abilities, embrace change, and not fear leaving their comfort zone.

dailyNews.title: Quote of the Day by Ray Dalio: Why investors chase the past
dailyNews.description: American billionnare Ray Dalio's quote is really a warning against recency bias—the habit of mistaking what has just worked for what will keep working.
